chief_complaint: A 60-year-old man presents with recurrent stroke.
history: The patient has a history of recurrent stroke, and histopathology is key to the diagnosis of Immunoglobulin G4-related disease (IgG4-RD).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Immunohistochemistry demonstrated scattered plasma cells in the intima and adventitia of the superficial temporal artery, with a small number of IgG4-positive cells observed (10-15 cells per high-power field).
